items.title: Trump to Take Up Manila’s Concerns With Xi, Philippines Says
items.description: US President Donald Trump assured Philippine leader Ferdinand Marcos Jr. that he would take up Manila’s concerns when he meets with Chinese President Xi Jinping, the Southeast Asian nation said.

items.title: PHP/USD: Philippine Peso Falls to Fresh Record Low as Oil Advances
items.description: The Philippine peso dropped to a new record low as oil prices climbed, putting pressure on the central bank to step up its defense of the currency.

items.title: Modi Seeks to Calm Anger in Rare Video as India Protests Widen
items.description: Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi made a fresh bid to calm mounting anger over examination paper leaks as students prepared nationwide protests on Friday, with demands growing for the education mini

items.title: PBOC Adds Most Liquidity to Economy in Five Months to Aid Growth
items.description: China’s central bank made its biggest addition of liquidity to the economy in five months via its medium-term lending facility to support growth ahead of its late July Politburo meeting and to accommo

items.title: Yen’s Worst Week Since May Brings It Close to 165 Versus Dollar
items.description: The yen is on track for its biggest weekly loss in more than two months, with intervention warnings doing little to deter investors betting on further weakness.

items.title: Singapore Pushes Back as US Levies Hit on Forced-Labor Claim
items.description: Singapore’s top diplomat said there was no economic justification for the US to impose tariffs on the city-state, adding that it seeks to avoid becoming “collateral damage” in Washington’s broader tra

items.title: S. Korea Moves Up Leveraged ETF Deposit Rule to Tame Volatility
items.description: South Korea is moving up the date it had planned to lift the minimum cash deposit requirement for investors in leveraged exchange-traded funds to July 31, a measure to curb demand for the products tha

items.title: CXMT’s Frenzy Ignites Hopes of Futher Hardware Rally in China
items.description: Investors are looking to Monday’s trading debut of CXMT Corp. to help renew a rally in Chinese semiconductor stocks that has been on pause ahead of the memory giant’s initial public offering.

items.title: Gold Holds Decline as Surging Energy Costs Raise Rate-Hike Bets
items.description: Gold held a retreat as the widening conflict in the Middle East pressured energy prices and increased expectations the US Federal Reserve will tighten monetary policy to contain inflation.

items.title: Japan Is Whipsawing Wall Street’s CLO Machine, Study Shows
items.description: The $1.3 trillion US CLO market is taking its cues from Tokyo as much as Wall Street.

items.title: Trive-Backed Lyntris Files For US IPO, Joining Defense Rush
items.description: Lyntris Inc., a defense technology firm backed by Trive Capital, filed for a US initial public offering.

items.title: Asian Stocks Fall on Tech, Brent Trades Above $100: Markets Wrap
items.description: Asian stocks followed Wall Street lower as investors grew skeptical that the billions being poured into the artificial intelligence buildout will generate adequate returns. Brent traded above $100 a b

items.title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 24
items.description: Brent crude traded above $100 a barrel after Houthi attacks on tankers in the Red Sea opened a new front in the Middle East conflict, while President Donald Trump threatened to extend US strikes on Ir

items.title: Advent to Hire Morgan Stanley to Sell Its Tigre Stake
items.description: Advent hired Morgan Stanley to explore a potential sale of its stake in Tigre SA, a Brazilian manufacturer of pipe and hydraulic systems, according to people familiar with the matter.

items.title: Overseas Buyers In Hot Pursuit of US Crude as Wars Escalate
items.description: An uptick in Asian and European demand for US crude is among the first signs of mounting concerns over adequate oil supplies amid multiplying geopolitical flashpoints.

items.title: GIC Posts Worst Five-Year Return Since 2013; US Still Top Market
items.description: Singaporean sovereign wealth fund GIC Pte reported its lowest five-year annualized return in more than a decade, after the giant investor reduced some of its risks earlier on and its bond holdings did

items.title: Goldman Offers Way to Trade AI Junk Bonds $250 Million at a Time
items.description: Goldman Sachs Group Inc. and JPMorgan Chase & Co. launched products this week allowing investors to quickly cut — or ramp up — exposure to tech industry debt, as concerns mount over hyperscalers’ huge

items.title: Wellington Is Passing on Data-Center Debt Deals on Property Value Concerns
items.description: Data-center debt risks are rising, there’s uncertainty over what the properties are worth and returns are falling, according to Wellington Management.

items.title: Tesla Short Sellers Rake In $4 Billion as Shares Nosedive
items.description: The deep plunge in Tesla Inc.’s stock on Thursday is turning into a major payday for its short sellers.

items.title: S&P 500 Suffers Worst Day in a Month as Oil Soars Over $100
items.description: US stocks tumbled, with the S&P 500 Index suffering its worst day in a month, as doubts about whether the debt-fueled artificial-intelligence investment boom will pay off dragged down big tech stocks.
